title: Room: the local database
objective: See how UserDao and AppDatabase give the app offline storage.

body.p: Room wraps SQLite, Android's built-in on-device database. You write an interface describing queries; Room generates the SQL-executing code at build time.
Without it, every screen reload would need a fresh network call. Room lets the app show something instantly from cache, then update once the network call returns.

lineNotes: @Dao — marks this interface for Room's code generator.
Flow<List<UserEntity>> — a live stream: the UI updates automatically whenever the table changes.
OnConflictStrategy.REPLACE — overwrite an existing row with the same id instead of crashing.

quiz.q: Why does getAllUsers() return a Flow instead of a plain List?
options: So the UI updates live whenever the underlying data changes
